brand.name: AlphaGraphics
brand.vertical: B2B/professional (print & marketing services) with a local/service franchise layer

summary: AlphaGraphics is a full-service B2B print and marketing-communications company delivered through 270+ locally owned franchise centers, offering graphic design, digital and offset printing, large-format signage, vehicle wraps, direct mail, promotional products, and branding. It serves SMB owners and marketing managers, plus corporate and franchisor teams who need consistent, high-quality branded materials on a recurring basis. Revenue is project-based and repeat-purchase rather than subscription, with the local franchise owner holding the customer relationship.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation by getting a new customer to their first-value moment — a finished product that beats online-printer quality, delivered on time — and orient them to the full AlphaGraphics range.
campaigns.trigger: First quote approved / first order placed at a location.
campaigns.segment: New customers; branch by first product category (business cards, signage, direct mail) and by SMB vs. corporate/portal account.
campaigns.rationale: AlphaGraphics has no systematic post-order nurture, so first-time buyers get an order confirmation and nothing else — leaving the 'aha' (a flawless, on-brand finished product) and the full service range uncommunicated right when goodwill is highest.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome the moment the order is placed: set production-process expectations, reassure on quality/color accuracy, and give one clear next action so the project moves without delay.
emails.subject: Your project's in good hands — here's what happens next
emails.preview: Proof, production, delivery — and your local team on it from start to finish.
emails.cta: Approve your proof to start production
keyPoints: Map the steps to delivery (proof approval, production, pickup/delivery) so they know what to expect and by when
Reassure on the differentiators they came for: exact brand-color match, local support, and on-time turnaround vs. online-only printers
Name their local franchise owner/design contact and invite a direct reply with any file or spec questions

campaigns.name: Quote-to-Order Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a submitted free quote/estimate into an approved, paid order before the prospect defaults to a cheaper online printer.
campaigns.trigger: Quote/estimate requested via online form but not yet approved.
campaigns.segment: Open quote requests; branch by product type and by whether a design consultation is still needed.
campaigns.rationale: The free quote is AlphaGraphics' universal entry point, but unconverted requests get at most a follow-up call and no email — and the #1 reason quotes stall is the price-vs-commodity-printer objection, which a single well-aimed email can directly answer.

emails.purpose: Re-surface the pending quote and tackle the price objection head-on by reframing value (color accuracy, turnaround, local support, complex work online printers can't do), then make approval frictionless.
emails.subject: About your quote — why the cheapest print rarely costs less
emails.preview: Reprints, wrong colors, missed deadlines: the hidden price of online-only printers.
keyPoints: Acknowledge the price comparison directly, then contrast against the real cost of online-printer reprints, off-brand colors, and missed campaign deadlines
Reinforce the local-partner advantages: a real person who knows their files, faster turnaround, and handling of custom/large-format work
Offer a quick call or free design consultation to remove any remaining friction before they approve

campaigns.name: Reorder Reminder & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Recapture predictable repeat purchases before the customer forgets or defaults to an online option, and expand the relationship into adjacent product categories.
campaigns.trigger: Time-based reorder window since last order (≈90 days for business cards/stationery; product-type dependent); usage/anniversary milestones.
campaigns.segment: Active repeat customers approaching a reorder window; branch by last product category to cross-sell adjacent services (signage, vehicle wraps, direct mail, promo products).
campaigns.rationale: Repeat purchase is AlphaGraphics' entire revenue engine, yet there is no triggered reorder reminder — the single highest-leverage gap for a brand whose churn is simply 'one-and-done customers who didn't think of us next time.'

emails.purpose: Prompt an effortless reorder of the same files at the reorder window, and use the moment to surface one adjacent category the customer likely doesn't know AlphaGraphics offers.
emails.subject: Running low on business cards? We've still got your files
emails.preview: Same brand colors, same specs — reorder in a couple of clicks.
keyPoints: One-click reorder of the exact previous job — files, colors, and specs already on hand, no re-setup
Introduce one relevant adjacent product (e.g., matching signage, banners, or direct mail) most first-category customers don't realize is available
Keep the local-partner tone: a quick reply or call gets it handled, no online form maze

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant accounts that haven't ordered in 6+ months and protect list deliverability by re-establishing the local relationship.
campaigns.trigger: No order in 6+ months (or primary local contact changed).
campaigns.segment: Dormant past customers; branch by what they previously ordered and whether their local owner/contact has changed.
campaigns.rationale: Dormant accounts get no re-engagement today, and the brand's biggest churn driver — forgetting AlphaGraphics for the next need — is exactly what a win-back addresses; win-back also recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts and cleans the list.

emails.purpose: Reopen the relationship warmly, remind them of the quality they got last time, and give a concrete reason to come back now (new capability, seasonal need, or a fresh local-team introduction).
emails.subject: It's been a while — your brand's been on our mind
emails.preview: New services, same on-time, on-brand work you remember.
keyPoints: Reference the type of work they ordered before and the quality/turnaround they experienced
Give a reason to return now: a newer capability (e.g., vehicle wraps, large-format signage) or an upcoming seasonal window (trade show, Q4, grand opening)
Re-introduce their local team or new owner and invite a direct reply to pick up where they left off

campaigns.name: Quote-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Keep cold or stalled quote requesters warm over a longer cycle with proof and education until they're ready to commit.
campaigns.trigger: Quote requested but not converted by the conversion sequence; or free design-guide / consultation download.
campaigns.segment: Unconverted leads below sales-readiness; branch by industry (retail, real estate, restaurant, non-profit) and by product interest.
campaigns.rationale: Some quote requesters aren't ready to buy immediately, and with no nurture they go cold and revert to online printers — a stage-matched proof-and-education drip keeps AlphaGraphics top-of-mind for the eventual deadline-driven need.

emails.purpose: Earn re-engagement with concrete social proof — a relatable local-business case showing real turnaround and color accuracy — to move an evaluation-stage lead toward reopening their quote.
emails.subject: How a local business got trade-show-ready in 48 hours
emails.preview: Tight deadline, exact brand match — what working with us actually looks like.
keyPoints: Tell a short, believable customer story matched to the lead's industry, emphasizing speed and exact brand-color match under deadline pressure
Contrast the supported, local experience against the no-help online-printer alternative they may be weighing
Low-friction invitation to revisit their existing quote or book a free design consultation
